project.title: cryo-EM study of human liver cell lysate
project.projectDescription: This is the proteomic analysis data of Human liver cell lysate subjected to cryo-EM study.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Human liver cell was lysed and fractionated by size exclusion chromatography. Two protein fractions were collected and subjected to protein digestion followed by LC-MS/MS analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Proteins were identified by comparing all of the experimental peptide MS/MS spectra against the UniProt human proteome database using a database search engine, MaxQuant (version 2.0.3.1). Carbamidomethylation of cysteine was set as a fixed modification, whereas variable modifications included oxidation of methionine to methionine sulfoxide and acetylation of N-terminal amino groups. For peptide/protein identification, strict trypsin specificity was applied, the minimum peptide length was set to 6, the maximum missed cleavage was set to 2, and the cutoff false discovery rate was set to 0.01.

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples]: There were 50 donors in the sample group
Characteristics[Age]: ages ranging between 5 and 83 years old.
Characteristics[AncestryCategory]: The ethnic profile of this group was Caucasian (44), African American (1), Asian (1) and Hispanic (4).
Characteristics[Modification]: Carbamidomethylationofcysteinewassetasafixedmodification,whereasvariablemodifications included oxidation of methionine to methionine sulfoxide and acetylation of N-terminal amino groups.
Comment[Instrument]: ThermoScientific Fusion Lumos mass spectrometry system
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: analyzed by LC-MS/MS using a ThermoScientific Fusion Lumos mass spectrometry system.
Comment[FractionationMethod]: enriched using a Superdex 200 column (GE Healthcare) equilibrated with 20 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5, and 100 mM NaCl.
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: the maximum missed cleavage was set to 2
